text: When you work with APIs, you send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most APIs return data in JSON format, which is easy to read and use in programming. Before using many APIs, you need authentication, often with an API key. This key is a secret code that identifies you. APIs often have rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. If you exceed this limit, you may be blocked temporarily. Good error handling is important. You should always check the server's response for errors like "404 Not Found" or "429 Too Many Requests".
